info.title: HyperDX External API
info.description: API for managing HyperDX alerts and dashboards

tags.name: Dashboards
tags.description: Endpoints for managing dashboards and their visualizations

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Endpoints for querying raw data from log and trace sources

AlertErrorType.description: Category of error recorded during alert execution.

AlertExecutionError.description: An error recorded during a recent alert execution.

by.description: User ID who silenced the alert.

type.description: Channel type. Must be "webhook" for webhook alerts.

tileId.description: Tile ID for tile-based alerts. Must be a line, stacked bar, or number type tile.

savedSearchId.description: Saved search ID for saved_search alerts.

groupBy.description: Group-by key for saved search alerts.

threshold.description: Threshold value for triggering the alert. For between and not_between threshold types, this is the lower bound.

thresholdMax.description: Upper bound for between and not_between threshold types. Required when thresholdType is between or not_between, must be >= threshold.

scheduleOffsetMinutes.description: Offset from the interval boundary in minutes. For example, 2 with a 5m interval evaluates windows at :02, :07, :12, etc. (UTC).

scheduleStartAt.description: Absolute UTC start time anchor. Alert windows start from this timestamp and repeat every interval.

source.description: Alert source type (tile-based or saved search).

note.description: Freeform note for the alert. Supports markdown formatting.
note.example: Threshold raised from 50 to 100 on 2026-01-15. See [runbook](https://wiki.example.com/runbook).

executionErrors.description: Errors recorded during the most recent alert execution, if any.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source for this series

aggFn.description: Aggregation function to use on the data

where.description: Filter condition in Lucene query syntax

whereLanguage.description: Query language used in the where clause

groupBy.description: Fields to group the results by

metricName.description: Name of the metric (for metric data sources)

ts_bucket.description: Timestamp of the data point (bucket start time)

group.description: Group by values if groupBy was specified

data.description: Array of data points for the series

hyperdxSettingPrefix.description: Optional prefix for HyperDX-specific ClickHouse settings. Must only contain alphanumeric characters and underscores.

isPrometheusEndpoint.description: Optional. When true, `host` is treated as a Prometheus-compatible API endpoint (e.g. Prometheus or Thanos) and PromQL queries are proxied to it. When false or omitted, `host` is a ClickHouse HTTP endpoint.

password.description: ClickHouse password. Never returned by the API.

hyperdxSettingPrefix.description: Optional prefix for HyperDX-specific ClickHouse settings. Must only contain alphanumeric characters and underscores.

isPrometheusEndpoint.description: Optional. When true, `host` is treated as a Prometheus-compatible API endpoint (e.g. Prometheus or Thanos) and PromQL queries are proxied to it. When false or omitted, `host` is a ClickHouse HTTP endpoint.

password.description: ClickHouse password. If omitted or empty, the existing password is kept.

hyperdxSettingPrefix.description: Optional prefix for HyperDX-specific ClickHouse settings. Set to null or an empty string to clear the existing value. If omitted, the existing value is kept.

isPrometheusEndpoint.description: Optional. When true, `host` is treated as a Prometheus-compatible API endpoint. When false or omitted, `host` is a ClickHouse HTTP endpoint. Omit to keep the existing value unchanged.

meta.description: Present only when one or more stored connections could not be serialized and were omitted from `data`.

skipped.description: Number of connections omitted from the response because they failed serialization.

skippedIds.description: IDs of the connections that were omitted.

NumberFormatOutput.description: Output format type (currency, percent, byte, time, number, data_rate, throughput, duration).

AggregationFunction.description: Aggregation function to apply to the field or metric value.

QueryLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause.

type.description: Filter type. Currently only "sql" is supported.

condition.description: SQL filter condition. For example use expressions in the form "column IN ('value')".

MetricDataType.description: Metric data type, only for metrics data sources.

TimeSeriesDisplayType.description: Visual representation type for the time series.

QuantileLevel.description: Percentile level; only valid when aggFn is "quantile".

output.description: Output format applied to the number.

decimalBytes.description: Use decimal bytes (1000) vs binary bytes (1024).

numericUnit.description: Numeric unit for data, data rate, or throughput formats.

ChartPaletteToken.description: Palette token used to color a number tile. Tokens reflow across light and dark themes, so raw hex values are not accepted.

BackgroundChart.description: Optional background trend sparkline drawn behind a number tile's value, derived from a time-bucketed version of the tile's query. Builder number tiles only (raw SQL number tiles have no time dimension to bucket).

color.description: Optional palette-token override for the sparkline. When unset the sparkline inherits the tile's static color.

NumericColorCondition.description: Color rule comparing the displayed value against a single numeric bound.

value.description: Numeric bound the displayed value is compared against. Only finite numbers are accepted (Infinity and NaN are rejected).

color.description: Color applied when the rule matches.

BetweenColorCondition.description: Color rule matching when the displayed value falls within an inclusive range.

value.description: Inclusive [min, max] range. Both bounds must be finite numbers.

color.description: Color applied when the rule matches.

EqualityColorCondition.description: Color rule matching when the displayed value equals (eq) or does not equal (neq) a number or string.

value.description: A finite number, or a string up to 200 characters, to compare for equality.

color.description: Color applied when the rule matches.

NumberTileColorCondition.description: A single conditional color rule for a number tile. Rules are evaluated in order and the last matching rule wins. When no rule matches, the static color applies, then the default text color. The number-tile editor surfaces numeric and equality operators only.

type.description: Series type discriminator. Must be "time" for time-series charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query

aggFn.description: Aggregation function to apply to the field or metric value

level.description: Percentile level for quantile aggregations (e.g., 0.95 for p95)

field.description: Column or expression to aggregate (required for most aggregation functions except count)

alias.description: Display name for the series in the chart

where.description: Filter query for the data (syntax depends on whereLanguage)

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause

groupBy.description: Fields to group results by (creates separate series for each group)

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

metricDataType.description: Metric data type, only for metrics data sources.

metricName.description: Metric name for metrics data sources

displayType.description: Visual representation type for the time series

type.description: Series type discriminator. Must be "table" for table charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query

aggFn.description: Aggregation function to apply to the field or metric value

level.description: Percentile level for quantile aggregations (e.g., 0.95 for p95)

field.description: Column or expression to aggregate (required for most aggregation functions except count)

where.description: Filter query for the data (syntax depends on whereLanguage)

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause

groupBy.description: Fields to group results by (creates separate rows for each group)

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

metricDataType.description: Metric data type, only for metrics data sources.

metricName.description: Metric name for metrics data sources

type.description: Series type discriminator. Must be "number" for single-value number charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query

aggFn.description: Aggregation function to apply to the field or metric value

level.description: Percentile level for quantile aggregations (e.g., 0.95 for p95)

field.description: Column or expression to aggregate (required for most aggregation functions except count)

alias.description: Display name for the series in the chart

where.description: Filter query for the data (syntax depends on whereLanguage)

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

metricDataType.description: Metric data type, only for metrics data sources.

metricName.description: Metric name for metrics data sources.

type.description: Series type discriminator. Must be "search" for search/log viewer charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query

fields.description: List of field names to display in the search results table

where.description: Filter query for the data (syntax depends on whereLanguage)

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause

type.description: Series type discriminator. Must be "markdown" for markdown text widgets.

content.description: Markdown content to render inside the widget.
content.example:
# Dashboard Title

This is a markdown widget.

SelectItem.description: A single aggregated value to compute. The valueExpression must be omitted when aggFn is "count", and required for all other functions. The level field may only be used with aggFn "quantile".

aggFn.description: Aggregation function to apply. "count" does not require a valueExpression; "quantile" requires a level field indicating the desired percentile (e.g., 0.95).

valueExpression.description: Expression for the column or value to aggregate. Must be omitted when aggFn is "count"; required for all other aggFn values.

alias.description: Display alias for this select item in chart legends.

level.description: Percentile level; only valid when aggFn is "quantile".

where.description: SQL or Lucene filter condition applied before aggregation.

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause.

metricName.description: Name of the metric to aggregate; only applicable when the source is a metrics source.

metricType.description: Metric type; only applicable when the source is a metrics source.

periodAggFn.description: Optional period aggregation function for Gauge metrics (e.g., compute the delta over the period).

numberFormat.description: Per-series number formatting options. When set, takes precedence over the chart-level numberFormat for this select item only.

LineBuilderChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a line time-series chart.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "line" for line charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: One or more aggregated values to plot. When asRatio is true, exactly two select items are required.

groupBy.description: Field expression to group results by (creates separate lines per group value).

asRatio.description: Plot select[0] / select[1] as a ratio. Requires exactly two select items.

alignDateRangeToGranularity.description: Expand date range boundaries to the query granularity interval.

fillNulls.description: Fill missing time buckets with zero instead of leaving gaps.

fitYAxisToData.description: Set the y-axis lower bound to the minimum of the displayed data instead of zero, making small fluctuations between series easier to see.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

compareToPreviousPeriod.description: Overlay the equivalent previous time period for comparison.

BarBuilderChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a stacked-bar time-series chart.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "stacked_bar" for stacked-bar charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: One or more aggregated values to plot. When asRatio is true, exactly two select items are required.

groupBy.description: Field expression to group results by (creates separate bars segments per group value).

asRatio.description: Plot select[0] / select[1] as a ratio. Requires exactly two select items.

alignDateRangeToGranularity.description: Align the date range boundaries to the query granularity interval.

fillNulls.description: Fill missing time buckets with zero instead of leaving gaps.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

TableBuilderChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a table aggregation chart.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "table" for table charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: One or more aggregated values to display as table columns. When asRatio is true, exactly two select items are required.

groupBy.description: Field expression to group results by (one row per group value).

orderBy.description: SQL ORDER BY expression for sorting table rows.

asRatio.description: Display select[0] / select[1] as a ratio. Requires exactly two select items.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

groupByColumnsOnLeft.description: When true, render Group By columns to the left of series columns in the table. Defaults to false (Group By columns on the right).

onClick.description: Optional link-out configuration applied when a user clicks a row.

NumberBuilderChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a single big-number chart.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "number" for single big-number charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: Exactly one aggregated value to display as a single number.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

color.description: Optional static color applied to the displayed number.

colorRules.description: Ordered conditional color rules evaluated against the displayed value (last match wins). Falls back to color, then the default text color when no rule matches.

backgroundChart.description: Optional background trend sparkline drawn behind the value.

PieBuilderChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a pie chart tile. Each slice represents one group value.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "pie" for pie charts.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: Exactly one aggregated value used to size each pie slice.

groupBy.description: Field expression to group results by (one slice per group value).

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

HeatmapSelectItem.description: Single select item for a heatmap tile. The value being bucketed is provided in valueExpression and the count contributing to each bucket in countExpression. The heatmap-specific fields (countExpression, heatmapScaleType) are persisted on the select item, not the chart config. The chart-level discriminator is the HeatmapChartConfig's `displayType: "heatmap"`; no aggregation function or alias is exposed on this select item because the heatmap aggregation function is fixed internally and the HeatmapSeriesEditor does not render an alias input.

valueExpression.description: SQL expression for the value being bucketed on the y-axis. Must be non-empty.

countExpression.description: SQL expression for the count contributing to each bucket. Defaults to "count()" in the editor when omitted.

heatmapScaleType.description: Scale type used to bucket values on the y-axis.

HeatmapChartConfig.description: Builder configuration for a heatmap tile. Heatmap is builder-only (no Raw SQL variant) and currently supports trace sources. The row-level filter lives at the chart-config level (where / whereLanguage), matching the HeatmapSeriesEditor in the UI.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "heatmap" for heatmap tiles.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

SearchChartConfig.description: Configuration for a raw-event search / log viewer tile.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "search" for search/log viewer tiles.

sourceId.description: ID of the data source to query.

select.description: Comma-separated list of expressions to display.

where.description: Filter condition for the search (syntax depends on whereLanguage).

whereLanguage.description: Query language for the where clause.

MarkdownChartConfig.description: Configuration for a freeform Markdown text tile.

displayType.description: Display type discriminator. Must be "markdown" for markdown text tiles.

markdown.description: Markdown content to render inside the tile.
markdown.example:
# Dashboard Title

This is a markdown widget.

RawSqlChartConfigBase.description: Shared fields for Raw SQL chart configs. Set configType to "sql" and provide connectionId + sqlTemplate instead of sourceId + select.

configType.description: Must be "sql" to use the Raw SQL chart config variant.

connectionId.description: ID of the ClickHouse connection to execute the query against.

sqlTemplate.description: SQL query template to execute. Supports HyperDX template variables.
sqlTemplate.example: SELECT count() FROM otel_logs WHERE timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR

sourceId.description: Optional ID of the data source associated with this Raw SQL chart. Used for applying dashboard filters.

numberFormat.description: Number formatting options for displayed values.

LineRawSqlChartConfig.description: Raw SQL configuration for a line time-series chart.

displayType.description: Display as a line time-series chart.

compareToPreviousPeriod.description: Overlay the equivalent previous time period for comparison.

fillNulls.description: Fill missing time buckets with zero instead of leaving gaps.

alignDateRangeToGranularity.description: Expand date range boundaries to the query granularity interval.

fitYAxisToData.description: Set the y-axis lower bound to the minimum of the displayed data instead of zero, making small fluctuations between series easier to see.

BarRawSqlChartConfig.description: Raw SQL configuration for a stacked-bar time-series chart.

displayType.description: Display as a stacked-bar time-series chart.

fillNulls.description: Fill missing time buckets with zero instead of leaving gaps.

alignDateRangeToGranularity.description: Expand date range boundaries to the query granularity interval.

TableRawSqlChartConfig.description: Raw SQL configuration for a table chart.

onClick.description: Optional link-out configuration applied when a user clicks a row.

NumberRawSqlChartConfig.description: Raw SQL configuration for a single big-number chart.

displayType.description: Display as a single big-number chart.

color.description: Optional static color applied to the displayed number. Raw SQL number tiles do not support conditional colorRules.

PieRawSqlChartConfig.description: Raw SQL configuration for a pie chart.

LineChartConfig.description: Line chart. Omit configType for the builder variant (requires sourceId and select). Set configType to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant (requires connectionId and sqlTemplate).

BarChartConfig.description: Stacked-bar chart. Omit configType for the builder variant (requires sourceId and select). Set configType to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant (requires connectionId and sqlTemplate).

OnClickFilterTemplate.description: A templated filter applied to the link-out destination. The rendered template value is combined with the expression as `expression IN (...)` on the destination search or dashboard. Multiple templates sharing the same expression are merged into a single IN clause.

kind.description: Filter template kind. Currently only "expressionTemplate" is supported.

expression.description: The column/expression to filter the destination by (e.g. "ServiceName").

template.description: Value template rendered against the clicked row; supports row column variables in `{{column}}` form (e.g. `{{ServiceName}}`).

OnClickTarget.description: Identifies the source (for type=search) or dashboard (for type=dashboard) to link out to. Set mode to "id" to resolve a concrete ID, or "template" to resolve by rendered name at click time.

mode.description: Target is a single dashboard or log/trace source

id.description: ID of the target source (for search) or dashboard (for dashboard).

mode.description: Target is matched by name against the template.

template.description: Name template rendered against the clicked row; supports `{{column}}` variables.

OnClickSearch.description: Link-out that navigates to the HyperDX search view.

type.description: OnClick variant discriminator. Must be "search" for search link-outs.

whereTemplate.description: Optional WHERE clause template applied to the destination search.

filters.description: Optional dashboard filter templates rendered against the clicked row.

OnClickDashboard.description: Link-out that navigates to a HyperDX dashboard.

type.description: OnClick variant discriminator. Must be "dashboard" for dashboard link-outs.

whereTemplate.description: Optional WHERE clause template applied to the destination dashboard.

filters.description: Optional dashboard filter templates rendered against the clicked row.

OnClickExternal.description: Link-out that navigates to an arbitrary external URL (e.g. a Grafana or Langfuse dashboard). The rendered URL must be an absolute http(s) URL.

type.description: OnClick variant discriminator. Must be "external" for external link-outs.

urlTemplate.description: Handlebars template rendered against the clicked row; supports `{{column}}` variables. The rendered value must be an absolute http(s) URL.

OnClick.description: Link-out configuration applied when a user clicks a row of a table tile. Only table tiles (builder or raw SQL) currently support onClick. When target.mode is "id", the referenced source (type=search) or dashboard (type=dashboard) must already exist for the team.

TableChartConfig.description: Table chart. Omit configType for the builder variant (requires sourceId and select). Set configType to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant (requires connectionId and sqlTemplate).

NumberChartConfig.description: Single big-number chart. Omit configType for the builder variant (requires sourceId and select). Set configType to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant (requires connectionId and sqlTemplate).

PieChartConfig.description: Pie chart. Omit configType for the builder variant (requires sourceId and select). Set configType to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant (requires connectionId and sqlTemplate).

TileConfig.description: Tile chart configuration. displayType is the primary discriminant and determines which variant group applies. For displayTypes that support both builder and Raw SQL modes (line, stacked_bar, table, number, pie), configType is the secondary discriminant: omit it for the builder variant or set it to "sql" for the Raw SQL variant. The heatmap, search, and markdown displayTypes only have a builder variant.

DashboardContainerTab.description: A single tab inside a dashboard container. Tiles join a tab via tabId.

id.description: Unique identifier for the tab within its container.

DashboardContainer.description: A grouping container for tiles on a dashboard. Tiles join a container via containerId.

id.description: Unique identifier for the container within the dashboard.

collapsed.description: Persisted default collapse state. Per-viewer state lives in the URL.

collapsible.description: Whether the user can collapse the group.

bordered.description: Whether to show a visual border around the group.

tabs.description: Optional tabs. 2+ entries renders a tab bar; 0-1 entries renders a plain group header. Tiles join a tab via tabId.

TileBase.description: Common fields shared by tile input and output

config.description: Chart configuration for the tile. The displayType field determines which variant is used. Replaces the deprecated "series" and "asRatio" fields.

containerId.description: References a DashboardContainer by id. Tiles without containerId render in the default ungrouped area.

tabId.description: References a tab inside the tile's container by id. Requires containerId to be set, and the container to declare a matching tab.

id.description: Unique tile ID assigned by the server.

TileInput.description: Input / request format when creating or updating tiles. The id field is optional: on create it is ignored (the server always assigns a new ID); on update, a matching id is used to identify the existing tile to preserve. Tiles whose id does not match an existing tile are assigned a new generated ID.

id.description: Optional tile ID. Omit to generate a new ID.

asRatio.description: Display two series as a ratio (series[0] / series[1]). Only applicable when providing "series". Deprecated in favor of "config.asRatio".

series.description: Data series to display in this tile (all must be the same type). Deprecated; use "config" instead.

FilterInput.description: Dashboard filter key that can be added to a dashboard

name.description: Display name for the dashboard filter key

expression.description: Key expression used when applying this dashboard filter key

sourceId.description: Source ID this dashboard filter key applies to

sourceMetricType.description: Metric type when source is metrics

where.description: Optional WHERE condition to scope which rows this filter key reads values from

appliesToSourceIds.description:
Optional list of source IDs this filter applies to. Omit or provide
an empty array to apply the filter to ALL tiles regardless of source.
A non-empty array restricts the filter to only tiles whose source ID
is in the list; tiles using other sources are not affected by the
selected filter value(s).

tiles.description: List of tiles/charts in the dashboard

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering dashboards

filters.description: Dashboard filter keys added to the dashboard and applied to all tiles

savedQuery.description: Optional default dashboard query restored when loading the dashboard.

savedFilterValues.description: Optional default dashboard filter values restored when loading the dashboard.

containers.description: Optional grouping containers. Each tile may join a container via tile.containerId, and a tab inside it via tile.tabId.

tiles.description: List of tiles/charts to include in the dashboard.

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering dashboards.

filters.description: Dashboard filter keys to add to the dashboard and apply across all tiles

savedQuery.description: Optional default dashboard query to persist on the dashboard.

savedFilterValues.description: Optional default dashboard filter values to persist on the dashboard.

containers.description: Optional grouping containers. Each tile may join a container via tile.containerId, and a tab inside it via tile.tabId.

tiles.description: Full list of tiles for the dashboard. Existing tiles are matched by ID; tiles with an ID that does not match an existing tile will be assigned a new generated ID.

tags.description: Tags for organizing and filtering dashboards.

filters.description: Dashboard filter keys on the dashboard, applied across all tiles

savedQuery.description: Optional default dashboard query to persist on the dashboard.

savedFilterValues.description: Optional default dashboard filter values to persist on the dashboard.

containers.description: Optional grouping containers. Each tile may join a container via tile.containerId, and a tab inside it via tile.tabId.

name.description: Display name for the saved search.

sourceId.description: ID of the source this saved search queries.

select.description: Comma-separated list of column expressions to display. Empty uses the source default.

where.description: Row filter expression. The language is controlled by whereLanguage.

whereLanguage.description: Language used for the where filter.

orderBy.description: ORDER BY expression. Empty uses the source default.

tags.description: Tags used to organize saved searches.

filters.description: Structured pinned filters applied to the search.

teamId.description: ID of the team that owns the saved search.

name.description: Display name for the saved search.

sourceId.description: ID of the source to query. Must belong to the team.

select.description: Comma-separated list of column expressions to display. Empty uses the source default.

where.description: Row filter expression. The language is controlled by whereLanguage.

whereLanguage.description: Language used for the where filter.

orderBy.description: ORDER BY expression. Empty uses the source default.

tags.description: Tags used to organize saved searches.

filters.description: Structured pinned filters applied to the search.

sourceId.description:
Source ID to query. Call GET /api/v2/sources to list available sources.
The source determines the underlying ClickHouse table (e.g. otel.otel_logs,
otel.otel_traces) and its column schema.

startTime.description: Start of the query window (ISO 8601). Defaults to 15 minutes before endTime. Must be before endTime.

endTime.description: End of the query window (ISO 8601). Defaults to now.

where.description:
Row filter expression. The language is controlled by whereLanguage.

Lucene examples (default):
  SeverityText:ERROR
  pipedream.pipeline_name:my-pipeline AND SeverityText:ERROR
  Body:timeout

SQL examples (whereLanguage: "sql"):
  SeverityText = 'ERROR'
  `pipedream.pipeline_name` = 'my-pipeline'

whereLanguage.description: Language used for the where filter. Default is lucene.

select.description:
Comma-separated list of ClickHouse column expressions to include in
each result row. When omitted the source's default select expression
is used.

Each entry is a ClickHouse SQL expression executed under the team's
database user. Semicolons and subqueries (SELECT keyword) are
rejected; use column references, map lookups, or function calls only.

HyperDX rewrites known attribute column names to their materialized
equivalents automatically; you can still pass the logical name.

orderBy.description:
ClickHouse ORDER BY expression. When omitted the source's default
ordering (typically timestamp DESC) is used.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Default is 100, max is 2000.

offset.description:
Number of rows to skip (best-effort offset pagination). Default is
0, max is 10000. Offset pagination is non-deterministic when
multiple rows share the same timestamp; for reliable deep paging
filter by the last Timestamp value returned in the previous page
instead of using a large offset.

SearchRow.description: A single result row. Keys correspond to the requested columns (or the source default columns when columns was omitted). Values are strings or numbers as returned by ClickHouse.

data.description: Array of result rows. Each row is an object with keys corresponding to the requested columns.

rows.description: Number of rows in this response (not total matching rows).

MetricTables.description: Mapping of metric data types to table names. At least one must be specified.

summary.description: Table containing summary metrics data. Note - not yet fully supported by HyperDX

exponential histogram.description: Table containing exponential histogram metrics data. Note - not yet fully supported by HyperDX

luceneExpression.description: An optional, Lucene version of the sqlExpression expression. If provided, it is used when searching for this attribute value.

databaseName.description: Database name for the materialized view

tableName.description: Table name for the materialized view

dimensionColumns.description: Columns which are not pre-aggregated in the materialized view and can be used for filtering and grouping.

minGranularity.description: The granularity of the timestamp column: a positive integer followed by a unit (s, m, h, d). Common values: 1s, 15s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 6h, 12h, 1d, 2d, 7d, 30d.

minDate.description: (Optional) The earliest date and time for which the materialized view contains data. If not provided, then HyperDX will assume that the materialized view contains data for all dates for which the source table contains data.

aggregatedColumns.description: Columns which are pre-aggregated by the materialized view

id.description: Unique source ID. Server-generated; ignored if sent in create/update requests.

section.description: Optional grouping label used to organize sources in the source selector. Sources that share a section value are displayed together.

disabled.description: When true, the source is hidden from source selectors in the UI. Defaults to false.

kind.description: Source kind discriminator. Must be "log" for log sources.

connection.description: ID of the ClickHouse connection used by this source.

from.description: Database and table location of the source data.

querySettings.description: Optional ClickHouse query settings applied when querying this source.

defaultTableSelectExpression.description: Default columns selected in search results (this can be customized per search later)

timestampValueExpression.description: DateTime column or expression that is part of your table's primary key.

serviceNameExpression.description: Expression to extract the service name from log rows.

severityTextExpression.description: Expression to extract the severity/log level text.

bodyExpression.description: Expression to extract the log message body.

displayedTimestampValueExpression.description: This DateTime column is used to display and order search results.

metricSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for metrics associated with logs. Optional

traceSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for traces associated with logs. Optional

traceIdExpression.description: Expression to extract the trace ID for correlating logs with traces.

spanIdExpression.description: Expression to extract the span ID for correlating logs with traces.

implicitColumnExpression.description: Column used for full text search if no property is specified in a Lucene-based search. Typically the message body of a log.

knownColumnsListExpression.description: For Distributed table sources whose target tables have non-matching column sets. A list of columns supported across all target tables, used instead of SELECT * when fetching full row data. Leave blank to select all columns.

useTextIndexForImplicitColumn.description: Controls whether lucene rendering uses ClickHouse text indices via hasAllTokens() against the implicit column. "auto" detects a covering index at query time, "enabled" forces text index usage, "disabled" forces a LIKE/hasToken fallback.

highlightedTraceAttributeExpressions.description: Expressions defining trace-level attributes which are displayed in the trace view for the selected trace.

highlightedRowAttributeExpressions.description: Expressions defining row-level attributes which are displayed in the row side panel for the selected row.

materializedViews.description: Configure materialized views for query optimization. These pre-aggregated views can significantly improve query performance on aggregation queries.

metadataMaterializedViews.description: Configure materialized views for fast field discovery and value autocomplete.

keyRollupTable.description: ClickHouse table name for the key rollup (field discovery).

kvRollupTable.description: ClickHouse table name for the key-value rollup (value autocomplete).

granularity.description: The time granularity of the rollup tables.

id.description: Unique source ID. Server-generated; ignored if sent in create/update requests.

section.description: Optional grouping label used to organize sources in the source selector. Sources that share a section value are displayed together.

disabled.description: When true, the source is hidden from source selectors in the UI. Defaults to false.

kind.description: Source kind discriminator. Must be "trace" for trace sources.

connection.description: ID of the ClickHouse connection used by this source.

from.description: Database and table location of the source data.

querySettings.description: Optional ClickHouse query settings applied when querying this source.

defaultTableSelectExpression.description: Default columns selected in search results (this can be customized per search later)

timestampValueExpression.description: DateTime column or expression defines the start of the span

durationPrecision.description: Number of decimal digits in the duration value (e.g., 3 for milliseconds, 6 for microseconds, 9 for nanoseconds).

traceIdExpression.description: Expression to extract the trace ID.

spanIdExpression.description: Expression to extract the span ID.

parentSpanIdExpression.description: Expression to extract the parent span ID.

spanNameExpression.description: Expression to extract the span name.

spanKindExpression.description: Expression to extract the span kind (e.g., client, server, internal).

logSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for logs associated with traces. Optional

sessionSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for sessions associated with traces. Optional

metricSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for metrics associated with traces. Optional

statusCodeExpression.description: Expression to extract the span status code.

statusMessageExpression.description: Expression to extract the span status message.

serviceNameExpression.description: Expression to extract the service name from trace rows.

spanEventsValueExpression.description: Expression to extract span events. Used to capture events associated with spans. Expected to be Nested ( Timestamp DateTime64(9), Name LowCardinality(String), Attributes Map(LowCardinality(String), String)

implicitColumnExpression.description: Column used for full text search if no property is specified in a Lucene-based search. Typically the message body of a log.

knownColumnsListExpression.description: For Distributed table sources whose target tables have non-matching column sets. A list of columns supported across all target tables, used instead of SELECT * when fetching full row data. Leave blank to select all columns.

useTextIndexForImplicitColumn.description: Controls whether lucene rendering uses ClickHouse text indices via hasAllTokens() against the implicit column. "auto" detects a covering index at query time, "enabled" forces text index usage, "disabled" forces a LIKE/hasToken fallback.

highlightedTraceAttributeExpressions.description: Expressions defining trace-level attributes which are displayed in the trace view for the selected trace.

highlightedRowAttributeExpressions.description: Expressions defining row-level attributes which are displayed in the row side panel for the selected row

materializedViews.description: Configure materialized views for query optimization. These pre-aggregated views can significantly improve query performance on aggregation queries.

metadataMaterializedViews.description: Configure materialized views for fast field discovery and value autocomplete.

keyRollupTable.description: ClickHouse table name for the key rollup (field discovery).

kvRollupTable.description: ClickHouse table name for the key-value rollup (value autocomplete).

granularity.description: The time granularity of the rollup tables.

id.description: Unique source ID. Server-generated; ignored if sent in create/update requests.

section.description: Optional grouping label used to organize sources in the source selector. Sources that share a section value are displayed together.

disabled.description: When true, the source is hidden from source selectors in the UI. Defaults to false.

kind.description: Source kind discriminator. Must be "metric" for metric sources.

connection.description: ID of the ClickHouse connection used by this source.

from.description: Database and optional table location of the metric source data.

querySettings.description: Optional ClickHouse query settings applied when querying this source.

metricTables.description: Mapping of metric data types to their respective table names.

timestampValueExpression.description: DateTime column or expression that is part of your table's primary key.

resourceAttributesExpression.description: Column containing resource attributes for metrics

logSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for logs associated with metrics. Optional

id.description: Unique source ID. Server-generated; ignored if sent in create/update requests.

section.description: Optional grouping label used to organize sources in the source selector. Sources that share a section value are displayed together.

disabled.description: When true, the source is hidden from source selectors in the UI. Defaults to false.

kind.description: Source kind discriminator. Must be "session" for session sources.

connection.description: ID of the ClickHouse connection used by this source.

from.description: Database and table location of the source data.

querySettings.description: Optional ClickHouse query settings applied when querying this source.

timestampValueExpression.description: DateTime column or expression that is part of your table's primary key.

traceSourceId.description: HyperDX Source for traces associated with sessions.

PromqlSource.description: A source backed by a Prometheus-compatible endpoint, queried with PromQL. The referenced connection should be a Prometheus connection (isPrometheusEndpoint set to true).

id.description: Unique source ID. Server-generated; ignored if sent in create/update requests.

section.description: Optional grouping label used to organize sources in the source selector. Sources that share a section value are displayed together.

disabled.description: When true, the source is hidden from source selectors in the UI. Defaults to false.

kind.description: Source kind discriminator. Must be "promql" for PromQL sources.

connection.description: ID of the connection used by this source. Should reference a Prometheus-compatible connection.

from.description: Required by the API for all source kinds; not used when querying a Prometheus endpoint (empty strings are not accepted — use placeholder values such as "default").

querySettings.description: Optional ClickHouse query settings applied when querying this source.

timestampValueExpression.description: Required by the API for all source kinds; not used when querying a Prometheus endpoint.

description.description: Webhook description, shown in the UI
description.example: Sends critical alerts to the #incidents channel

url.description: incident.io alert event HTTP source URL

description.description: Webhook description, shown in the UI
description.example: Routes alerts to incident.io for on-call escalation

description.description: Webhook description, shown in the UI
description.example: Forwards alert payloads to an external monitoring service

WebhookInput.description:
Webhook create/update body. `headers` and `queryParams` are
write-only — they are accepted here but never returned by any
read endpoint, so secrets such as auth tokens do not leak. On
update (PUT), omitted readable fields (`description`, `body`) are
cleared, while omitted `headers`/`queryParams` are preserved —
send an explicit `{}` to clear them.

name.description: Webhook name. Must be unique per service within the team.

description.description: Webhook description, shown in the UI.
description.example: Sends critical alerts to the #incidents channel

headers.description: Write-only. Custom HTTP headers sent with the webhook request. Never returned on read.

queryParams.description: Write-only. Query parameters appended to the webhook URL. Never returned on read.

Field semantics: if `password` is omitted or empty the existing
password is kept. `hyperdxSettingPrefix` is cleared when set to null
or an empty string, and `prometheusEndpoint` is cleared when set to
null; both are kept unchanged when omitted.

get.description: Retrieves a specific dashboard by ID

example.message: Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.
Dashboard not found

put.description:
Updates an existing dashboard.

**Concurrency:** This endpoint does not support optimistic
concurrency control. Concurrent PUT requests for the same
dashboard may silently overwrite each other, which can leave
orphan tile-to-container references on layout-shape edits.
Clients should serialize edits to a given dashboard.

example.message: Could not find the following source IDs: 68fa86308aa879b977aa6af6
Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.
Dashboard not found
Invalid dashboard configuration
Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.
Dashboard not found

post.description:
Validates a dashboard body against the same schema and tile rules used
by POST /api/v2/dashboards. The dashboard is **never persisted**. Use
this endpoint at plan time (e.g. from a Terraform provider) to check
that a dashboard configuration is valid before applying it.

200.description:
Validation result. HTTP 200 is always returned for valid **and**
invalid bodies — a non-200 response means the request itself
failed (auth, server error, etc.).

valid.description: True when the body passes all validation rules.

errors.description: Validation errors. Empty when valid is true.

path.description: Dot-separated field path, or empty string for top-level errors.

normalized.description:
The parsed dashboard body with defaults applied (no
persistence, so no server-assigned tile IDs). Populated
when valid is true, null when valid is false.

example.message: Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.

get.description: Retrieves saved searches for the authenticated team.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of saved searches to return.

get.description: Retrieves a specific saved search by ID.

put.description:
Updates an existing saved search. Send the full object: `name`,
`sourceId`, `select`, `where`, `whereLanguage`, and `tags` are always
written (they fall back to their defaults when omitted). Omitting the
optional `orderBy` or `filters` leaves the stored value unchanged
rather than clearing it.

delete.description: Deletes a saved search and any alerts attached to it.

post.description:
Fetch individual log or trace rows from a HyperDX source.

This endpoint mirrors the "search" panel mode in the HyperDX UI.
HyperDX applies the same query optimizations used in the UI:
  - Named attribute columns (e.g. "pipedream.pipeline_name") are
    rewritten to their indexed materialized equivalents when the
    source schema exposes them, avoiding slow Map lookups.
  - Rows are ordered by timestamp descending (most recent first).
  - The source's built-in PREWHERE / partition pruning is applied.

Authentication: Bearer token (personal API key from Team Settings).

pipedreamTaskLogs.summary: Pipedream task logs with materialized column filter

400.description: Invalid request parameters or query error

get.description: Retrieves a list of all sources for the authenticated team

example.message: Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.

post.description:
Creates a new source.

The request body is a source object without the `id` field. If an
`id` is sent anyway it is silently ignored (stripped before
validation — the request is never rejected because of it).
Granularity fields
(`materializedViews[].minGranularity` and
`metadataMaterializedViews.granularity`) accept the same short format
the API returns (e.g. `5m`, `15s`, `1h`, `1d`).

example.message: Body validation failed: name: Required
Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.

get.description: Retrieves a specific source by ID

example.message: Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.
Source not found

put.description:
Updates an existing source. The full source object must be provided;
this is a replace, not a patch.

The request body is a source object without the `id` field. If an
`id` is sent anyway it is silently ignored (stripped before
validation — never a 400); the path parameter alone identifies the
source. Granularity fields (`materializedViews[].minGranularity` and
`metadataMaterializedViews.granularity`) accept the same short format
the API returns (e.g. `5m`, `15s`, `1h`, `1d`).

Unauthorized access. API key is missing or invalid.

post.description: Creates a new webhook for the authenticated team.

put.description:
Replaces an existing webhook. Readable optional fields
(`description`, `body`) are a full replace: omitting them clears
them. The write-only fields `headers` and `queryParams` are never
returned on read, so omitting them preserves the stored values;
send an explicit empty object (`{}`) to clear them.
